geography: TX — Houston/Dallas/Fort Worth/Austin/San Antonio + border (El Paso/RGV) + commercial corridors
thesis: LOW-PE-ATTENTION vertical. No major national PE platform consolidating; franchise systems (FastSigns/Signs Now/SpeedPro/Image360) + regional consolidators (YESCO Service Network, Signage Solutions, Stewart Signs) only. Growing ETA/search-fund appetite at favorable entry multiples (3-5x vs 5-8x in hotter trades). Target = independent multi-service shops (design+fab+install+maintenance+service routes), 15+ year tenure, aging boomer/silent-gen owners, B2B repeat customers, quiet recurring cash flow.

a_tier_deep_dive.note: Both A-tier candidates (AAA Electrical Signs, Global Signs) passed all 9 deep-dive items. Items 3 (Comptroller franchise-tax), 5 (county clerk civil filings), 6 (CAD building ownership) deferred — interactive form lookups not feasible via WebFetch this run. Recommend these be completed before any personal outreach. See signage_deep_dive.json for full per-item evidence.

filters_applied: TX counties only (exclude OUT OF STATE)
Exclude franchise units (FastSigns, Signs Now, SpeedPro, Image360, Sign-A-Rama, Alphagraphics)
Exclude platform subsidiaries (Stratus/Comet, YESCO, Stewart, Persona, Daktronics, Watchfire)
Exclude obvious non-sign names (Roofing, Plumbing, HVAC)
Dedupe by (legal_name, city) then by phone; kept older license number on ties
Sort by priority county (Harris/Dallas/Tarrant/Travis/Bexar/border + suburbs/coastal) then ascending license # (older = better)
Cap at 100 prioritizing longest-tenure rows in priority counties
